ingredients.name: Sodium butyrate
ingredients.rationale: Butyrate (~20 mM) as syntroph donor; oxidized via β-oxidation.

ingredients.name: Yeast extract
ingredients.rationale: Yeast extract supplies vitamins / growth factors.

ingredients.name: REQUIRED CO-CULTURE: hydrogenotrophic methanogen partner
ingredients.rationale: Pure-culture growth is thermodynamically impossible. Co-inoculate with Methanospirillum hungatei, Methanobacterium formicicum, or similar. The partner consumes H2 produced by syntroph fatty-acid oxidation, making the reaction exergonic.

ingredients.name: Phosphate buffer (KH2PO4 + K2HPO4)
ingredients.rationale: 30 mM phosphate buffer adjusted to pH 7.0

ingredients.name: NaCl
ingredients.rationale: Standard salinity baseline (5 g/L) for non-halophile

ingredients.name: SL-10 trace metal solution
ingredients.rationale: SL-10 (Tschech & Pfennig 1984): Fe, Zn, Mn, B, Co, Cu, Ni, Mo at standard concentrations. 1 mL/L final.

ingredients.name: Wolin's vitamin solution
ingredients.rationale: Wolin's vitamins (Wolin et al. 1963): biotin, folic acid, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboflavin, nicotinic acid, pantothenate, B12, p-aminobenzoic acid, lipoic acid. 1 mL/L final.

gas_phase.rationale: N2/CO2 anaerobic headspace; partner methanogen produces CH4.

thermodynamic_checks.primary_reaction: butyrate + 2 H2O → 2 acetate + 2 H2 + H+ (requires H2-consuming partner)
thermodynamic_checks.notes: Reaction is endergonic alone (ΔG > 0). Becomes feasible only when [H2] is kept low by a syntrophic partner — recipe explicitly requires partner co-inoculation.

composition_rationale: Syntrophy: organic donor oxidation is endergonic alone; only viable with a H2- or formate-consuming partner that keeps interspecies-H2 low. Recipe must include the partner (typically a hydrogenotrophic methanogen) — IT IS NOT POSSIBLE TO GROW PURE SYNTROPHS WITHOUT THEM.
uncertainty_flags: Partner organism MUST be added — pure-culture growth is endergonic
confidence_rationale: base from RecipeContext = 0.70; -0.05 from uncertainty flags → final 0.65
